title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: Tonight I am going to show you my night routine.
I've already got my PJs on and now I'm going to wash my face and take all of my makeup off.
I've finished with my oil cleanser and I'm going to rinse it off with warm water.
Now I'm going to go in with my foam cleanser.
My skin is dry and now I'm going to use a toner and I just rub it again all over my face to make sure that there is no makeup at all.

title: will / shall
examples: I think later I'll be very happy that I'm taking care of my skin now.
I put a bag of peppermint tea in my mug and now I'll pour the water into the mug.
I'll take my hair out and on the bonnet.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: I just filled the electric kettle with water so that we can boil water for my tea.
As you can see I've got a stack of books over here.
Maybe you can let me know in the comments what it means to you.
I think my tea is finally cool enough that I can take my first sip.
You can still see steam coming up from it though.

title: must / can't (deduction)
examples: Two things I must have when I sleep are my bonnet.

title: have to / must
examples: First I have to remove my eyeliner and mascara so I use some remover with a cotton pad.
So I have to do it.
I have to force myself to do it.
First I have to think and reflect a little bit because this is not an easy question.

title: had better / it's time
examples: After I remove my eye makeup it's time to cleanse my skin.

title: have something done
examples: Today was a really great but long day so I hope I get a good night's sleep and I hope I don't dream too much.
